title: Implement SSH RUN mount
body:
Allow ssh socket from host or certain ssh keys to be exposed to a
certain RUN instruction, but not any other instructions, as well as not
showing up in the final image.
This is done by spawining a new agent from buildah and mounting
the listening socket inside the run. SSH_AUTH_SOCK inside the container
will be set to the socket mountpoint. The defualt mountpoint is
/run/buildkit/ssh_agent.{i}

Signed-off-by: Ashley Cui <acui@redhat.com>

<!--
Thanks for sending a pull request!

Please make sure you've read and understood our contributing guidelines
(https://github.com/containers/buildah/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md) as well as ensuring
that all your commits are signed with `git commit -s`.
-->

#### What type of PR is this?
/kind feature
#### What this PR does / why we need it:
Allow ssh socket from host or certain ssh keys to be exposed to a
certain RUN instruction, but not any other instructions, as well as not
showing up in the final image.
This is done by spawining a new agent from buildah and mounting
the listening socket inside the run. SSH_AUTH_SOCK inside the container
will be set to the socket mountpoint. The defualt mountpoint is
/run/buildkit/ssh_agent.{i}

#### How to verify it
buildah bud --ssh id=default . 

RUN --mount=type=ssh,id=default

#### Which issue(s) this PR fixes:
Fixes #2835

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?

```release-note
Support SSH mounts in RUN instruction
```

comments.body: WIP because CI might get funky on this one since I'm creating SSH Keys. Also, if there's a better way than having to install openssh-client on every test run via the Dockerfile, let me know since that seems like something that can maybe be cached? I think there's also another SELinux bug here but I'll ask @rhatdan again when he comes back from the beach :)

the expected does show up as a substring of actual, but it seems flakey.

Here's the strange error that I'm getting: 
```
STEP 4/4: RUN --mount=type=ssh,id=default ssh-add -l; ssh git@github.com
3072 SHA256:UP+1IIyKqBfXHqlLaE9JO8L088FKOy+M3aHyf+gBgK4 acui@fedora (RSA)
256 SHA256:YkQgBsosvLxPjxjLVl18XIEMytqodMIYYFDQg9aVQcY root@fedora (ED25519)
256 SHA256:svQtLIoRuxW7kdSWlwQwWCuybH/DchNZPopAGBpzEMM acui@redhat.com (ED25519)
Pseudo-terminal will not be allocated because stdin is not a terminal.
ERRO[0005] error serving agent: read unix /var/tmp/.buildah-ssh-sock421179333/ssh_auth_sock->@: use of closed network connection 
sign_and_send_pubkey: signing failed for RSA "acui@fedora" from agent: communication with agent failed
```

It seems strange because ssh-add doesn't fail but the actual ssh command does. both should be using  the sock but it only throws failed on the ssh and not the ssh-add
I think the problem could be about Pseudo-terminal will not be allocated because stdin is not a terminal. could you try with a scp rather then an ssh?
I found it! It was just that my timeout was too short. I changed it to 500ms.

nice work @ashley-cui , take tomorrow off!
@ashley-cui @rhatdan should we backport this to release-1.22?
I'll let @rhatdan decide that one, but we have to be careful when this gets into podman, since this won't work with remote build
No, let's sit on this for a while.

Not sure if this is the correct place to document this, but here it goes. If using the (very nice!) SSH RUN feature and trying to clone from github, you will inevitably get the error

```Host key verification failed.
fatal: Could not read from remote repository.

Please make sure you have the correct access rights
and the repository exists.
```

I think the issue here is that git is trying to show a message like this:

```Cloning into '<dir>'...
The authenticity of host 'github.com (140.82.121.3)' can't be established.
ECDSA key fingerprint is SHA256:<fingerprint>.
Are you sure you want to continue connecting (yes/no/[fingerprint])? 
```

but this is probably failing due to how docker/podman works (maybe? I'm mostly guessing here).

The fix is to manually download the public key from github before trying to clone, for example via

    RUN mkdir -p -m 0600 ~/.ssh && ssh-keyscan github.com >> ~/.ssh/known_hosts

Sorry if this is not the correct place to post this.
